items.title: Hospital readmission with a leaky discharge flag
items.scenario: A classifier predicts 30-day readmission using EHR features. The team includes `discharge_to_hospice` as a feature. Hospice discharges rarely return within 30 days, so the model scores 98% AUC on the test set.
items.explanation: The feature encodes post-outcome care decisions correlated with the label horizon. At admission time you would not know hospice disposition the same way. Remove or redefine features to reflect only information available at prediction time.

items.title: Scaling before splitting house prices
items.scenario: An analyst runs `StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)` on all 50,000 listings, then does `train_test_split`. Test-set RMSE on price prediction looks excellent compared to a baseline that scales after the split.
items.explanation: The scaler learned mean and variance using test rows. That is preprocessing leakage. Fit the scaler on train only, then `transform` validation and test with those train-fitted statistics.

items.title: Stock return prediction with tomorrow's open
items.scenario: A quant team builds features including `next_day_open_gap` to predict whether today's close will rise. Backtest Sharpe ratio is 3.2; paper trading loses money immediately.
items.explanation: The feature uses tomorrow's open, which is unknown when the close prediction must be made. This is temporal leakage. Rebuild features using only prices and news available at or before the decision timestamp.

items.title: Duplicate users in train and test
items.scenario: A churn model splits rows randomly. The same `user_id` appears in both train and test because one user has many monthly rows. Offline accuracy is high; the production model for new months underperforms.
items.explanation: Row-level random split leaked near-copies of the same user into both sets. Use group-aware splits (all rows for a user in one partition) or split by time per user.
